zh.lifeSummary: 當Catharina Bach於1350年4月12日出生在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 奧斯包格時，她的父親Heinrich Bach III.40歲，母親Anna Langenmantel von Sparren36歲。她於1365年3月10日與Johann Rehm在德國貝也奧斯包格奧斯包格結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1411年9月1日在她的家鄉享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Catharina Bach于1350年4月12日出生在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 奧斯包格时，她的父亲Heinrich Bach III.40岁，母亲Anna Langenmantel von Sparren36岁。她于1365年3月10日与Johann Rehm在德国貝也奧斯包格奧斯包格结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1411年9月1日在她的家乡享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bach
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans, Kurt, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Johann, Alfons, Bernhard, Erna, Ernst.

German: topographic name for someone who lived by a stream, Middle High German bach ‘stream’. This surname is also found in some other European countries, most notably in Denmark and France (Alsace and Lorraine), but also in e.g. the Netherlands, Czechia (see also 5 below), and Croatia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Bach ‘stream, creek’.

variants.name: Bage
meanings: English (Durham and Yorkshire): possibly from Middle English bagge, Anglo-Norman French bage ‘badge, cognizance, emblem’, perhaps denoting someone who wore one as a sign of affiliation to a family or guild.
English: habitational name from The Bage in Dorstone, The Bage Farm in Madley, or Bage in Kington, all in Herefordshire. These are all names derived from Old English bæce ‘stream’; compare Bach 3.
French: variant of Bague, from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago, a short form of compound names based on the element bag ‘to fight’.

variants.name: Batch
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Bach   3.

variants.name: Baughan
meanings: Welsh: from the adjective bychan ‘little’, a diminutive of bach (see Bach 4), originally in general used to distinguish father and son bearing the same name in genealogies. This word usually appears in Anglicized surnames in the lenited form Vaughan .
History: A Welsh family of this name was established from an early date in Great Rollright, Oxfordshire, England. The name was probably brought to North America from Oxfordshire rather than Wales.

variants.name: Bartholomew
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

variants.name: Baugh
meanings: Welsh: probably an Anglicized form of Bach , from Welsh bach ‘little’.
Americanized form of German   Bach  .

variants.name: Baechle
meanings: South German (Bächle): Swabian-Alemannic variant of Bach , from a diminutive of Middle High German bach ‘stream’. Compare Bachle and Beckley .

variants.name: Bash
meanings: English: variant of   Bach   3.

Jewish: acronymic surname from the first letters of Hebrew ben ‘son of’ and of a personal name such as Shelomo (see Solomon ), Shemuel (see Samuel ), Shimon (see Simon ) or Shimshon (see Samson ); see also Brill . In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the variant Basch .
Americanized form of German   Basch  .

variants.name: Bache
meanings: German and English: variant of   Bach   1, 3.
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Bartholomaeus (see Bach 6, Bartholomew ).
East German: occupational name for a butcher from Middle High German bache ‘ham, smoked slab of bacon’.

variants.name: Bachofer
meanings: German: topographic name for a farmer living by a stream (see   Bach   and   Hofer  ).

nameMeanings.name: Catharina
meanings: English: An English form of Catharine.

en.lifeSummary: When Catharina Bach was born on 12 April 1350, in Augsburg, Bavaria, Germany, her father, Heinrich Bach III., was 40 and her mother, Anna Langenmantel von Sparren, was 36. She married Johann Rehm on 10 March 1365, in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 1 September 1411, in her hometown, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Catharina Bach est née le 12 avril 1350, à Landkreis Augsburg, Bayern, Allemagne, son père, Heinrich Bach III., avait 40 ans et sa mère, Anna Langenmantel von Sparren, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Johann Rehm le 10 mars 1365, à Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 septembre 1411, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Catharina Bach am 12. April 1350 in Landkreis Augsburg, Bayern, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Heinrich Bach III. 40 und ihre Mutter Anna Langenmantel von Sparren 36. Sie hat am 10. März 1365 in Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Deutschland Johann Rehm geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. September 1411 im Alter von 61 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Catharina Bach è nata il il 12 aprile 1350 nel Landkreis Augsburg, Bayern, Germania, sua padre, Heinrich Bach III., aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Anna Langenmantel von Sparren, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato Johann Rehm il 10 marzo 1365, nel Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 1 settembre 1411, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Catharina Bachが1350年4月12日にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス アウグスブルグで生まれた時，父Heinrich Bach III.は40歳，母Anna Langenmantel von Sparrenは36歳であった。1365年3月10日にドイツバイエルンアウグスブルグアウグスブルグでJohann Rehmと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1411年9月1日に故郷で61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Catharina Bach(이)가 1350년 4월 12일 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 오그스버그에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Heinrich Bach III.(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anna Langenmantel von Sparren(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1365년 3월 10일 독일 베이른 오그스버그 오그스버그에서 Johann Rehm(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1411년 9월 1일 그녀의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.12. April 1350–1. September 1411: 1350 оны Дөрөвдүгээр сарын 12–1411 оны Есдүгээр сарын 1

pt.12. April 1350–1. September 1411: 12 de abril de 1350–1 de setembro de 1411
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Catharina Bach nasceu em 12 de abril de 1350, em Landkreis Augsburg, Bayern, Alemanha, seu pai, Heinrich Bach III., tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Anna Langenmantel von Sparren, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com Johann Rehm em 10 de março de 1365, em Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de setembro de 1411, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Catharina Bach родилась 12 апреля 1350 г. в местности ландкрис аугсбург, беиирн, Германия, её отцу по имени Heinrich Bach III. было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Anna Langenmantel von Sparren, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Johann Rehm 10 марта 1365 г. в местности аугсбург, аугсбург, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 1 сентября 1411 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 61 год.

es.12. April 1350–1. September 1411: 12 de abril de 1350–1 de septiembre de 1411
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Catharina Bach nació el 12 de abril de 1350, en Landkreis Augsburg, Bayern, Alemania, su papá, Heinrich Bach III., tenía 40 años y su mamá, Anna Langenmantel von Sparren, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Johann Rehm el 10 de marzo de 1365, en Augsburg, Augsburg, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 1 de septiembre de 1411, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años.
